channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI-generated fake nudes now part of Russian cybercrime group's method
item.description: Cybercrime group Fin7 has created at least seven websites serving malware through generators that create fake nude images.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: How rockets could travel to Mars in half the time with nuclear thermal propulsion
item.description: NASA plans to send crewed missions to Mars over the next decade – but the 140 million-mile (225 million-kilometer) journey to the red planet could take several months to years round trip. This relatively long transit time is a result of the use of traditional chemical rocket fuel.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: NYT Strands today — hints, answers and spangram for Sunday, October 6 (game #217)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: The shocking rise and fall of elite cybersecurity firm IronNet
item.description: The future was once dazzling for IronNet. Founded by a former director of the National Security Agency and stacked with elite members of the U.S. intelligence establishment, IronNet promised it was going to revolutionize the way governments and corporations combat cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: We’ve entered the AI grift era
item.description: For years, Y Combinator has been revered as a highly selective startup accelerator with a hit rate that far exceeds the law of averages. But one of its latest companies has raised a few eyebrows, and prompted questions about whether AI grifters have caught up with the innovators.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers are flooding Trump’s new crypto exchange
item.description: Back in September, Donald Trump announced his new crypto exchange, World Liberty Financial (WLFI). Now, registered investors can officially sign up for the whitelist—assuming they can avoid the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Austrian activist Schrems wins privacy case against Meta over personal data on sexual orientation
item.description: The European Union’s top court has said that social media company Meta can’t use public information about a user’s sexual orientation obtained outside its platforms for personalized advertising under the bloc’s strict data privacy rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Does your SMB have the foundation in place for GenAI?
item.description: The extraordinary potential of generative AI (GenAI) has seen businesses scrambling to adopt the technology and realize untapped opportunities. But building an AI strategy is more than just deploying the newest GenAI tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence
item.description: How is the Army leveraging OSINT to strengthen its intelligence capabilities? The post OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple accused of violating labor laws, again
item.description: Apple has been accused of violating union rights, according to a complaint filed by the US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) .
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI raises $6.6 billion in fresh funding as it moves away from its nonprofit roots
item.description: OpenAI says it has raised $6.6 billion in venture capital investments as part of a broader shift by the ChatGPT maker away from its nonprofit roots.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information
item.description: The IG said CISA made some good improvements to the Automated Indicator Sharing program in recent years. The post CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk sending Starlink terminals to storm-ravaged North Carolina
item.description: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk said Tuesday the aerospace company is sending more Starlink terminals to North Carolina following the widespread devastation of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft unveils new AI-powered Windows 11 features for Copilot+ PC users
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday unveiled the latest AI features for Copilot+ PCs, with improved Windows search and a tool that can detect and act on the contents of a user’s screen.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 easy ways to transfer files between Windows and your phone
item.description: We live in a multi-device world. At a minimum, you almost certainly have both a computer and a phone — and potentially many more devices than that! And when it comes to transferring files between your Windows PC and whatever phone you’re using, you’re absolutely spoiled for choice.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google just made a major ChromeOS misstep
item.description: Did you ever watch the early 2000s TV series Scrubs? The show revolved around a young doctor named JD who always meant well but often got lost in his own head and ended up making ill-advised decisions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Breaking through AI data bottlenecks
item.description: As AI models become increasingly commoditized, the data required to train and fine-tune them has never been more critical.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: X's value has fallen 79% since Musk takeover, Fidelity estimates
item.description: Fidelity, which originally invested $19.66 million, now values its stake in the company at $4.19 million.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation
